content.rendered:
“Disgusted” was recorded by Melvin “Lil’ Son” Jackson back in 1951, but it remained in the vault for years, finally seeing the light of day on a 1968 compilation album.

Lucinda Williams has covered it in concert over the decades, too many times to count; she even included a live performance of it on as a bonus track on her 1998 reissue of her self-titled third album.

When Lucinda’s 2006 tour intersected with Bruce’s Seeger Sessions Tour in London, Bruce made a surprise guest appearance, joining Lucinda’s band for two songs. One of those songs–Lucinda’s own “Joy”–has been floating around Youtube for many years.

The other song, however, is much harder to find for some reason. So here it is, newly uploaded–enjoy Bruce’s only known performance of “Disgusted.” (He plays guitar only, no vocals.)

Disgusted
First performed: November 10, 2006 (London, England)
Last performed: November 10, 2006 (London, England)

© December 4, 2018

excerpt.rendered: One time only: Bruce joined Lucinda Williams and her band on stage in London to perform a cover of Melvin Jackson’s “Disgusted.”

aioseo_breadcrumb: Home » Cover Me » Disgusted

jetpack-related-posts.title: Cover Me, Lucinda Williams: Factory
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Lucinda Williams covers Bruce in concert often, but she's only covered him in the studio once. Take a listen to her version of "Factory" from her 2016 album, The Ghost of Highway 20.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Kingdom of Days: November 10
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Not one but *two* Springsteen box sets were released on this date: Live 1975-85 and Tracks. Plus: a Billy Joel guest appearance, a Daily Show interview, and more.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Long Walk Home
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: "Long Walk Home" lost its way in the studio, but in its original and acoustic arrangements, it's one of Bruce's finest and most relevant 21st century songs.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Cover Me: She Don’t Love Me Now
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: A return to form for Bruce as a cover artist and a fitting tribute for an artist we've featured here may times before.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Cover Me, The Deep Dark Woods: Factory
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Canadian band Deep Dark Woods turns in a faithful cover of Bruce's minimalist musing on the meaning of work.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Introduction
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: More than a decade on, Bruce's seventeenth studio album stands as one of his very best–and the strongest E Street Band album to date.
